This stage runs early in startup as cross-cutting discovery infrastructure: before configuration and execution decisions can be trusted, the process must know where Codex is installed, where its writable home lives, what resources are bundled, and what kind of local machine and shell it is operating in. utils/home-dir establishes the absolute CODEX_HOME location with strict validation for explicit overrides, while install-context identifies the installation layout, standalone release status, bundled assets, and helper binaries such as rg or zsh. managed_install adds managed-install specific knowledge, including binary resolution, versioning, and executable fingerprinting used by updater logic.

That filesystem context feeds helper_materialization, which copies packaged helper executables into a shared sandbox bin under codex_home with versioned names and freshness checks. shell_snapshot captures and later cleans up shell state for local sessions, and exec-server’s environment and environment_provider modules turn those snapshots and legacy URL-based inputs into validated, cached runtime environment objects for local or remote execution. Supporting probes round out the picture: host_name normalizes the machine identity, cloud env detection infers repository cloud environments, and the doctor modules report Git, runtime provenance, locale, editor, pager, and related platform facts for diagnostics.
